failure_modes.component: knee actuator harmonic drive
failure_modes.symptom: torque ripple and audible grinding under load, joint position error exceeding 2 degrees, gait instability on inclines
failure_modes.root_cause: harmonic drive flexspline fatigue from cyclical high-torque loading without lubrication interval compliance; exacerbated by operation on uneven terrain increasing peak torque spikes

repair_protocol.title: Knee Actuator Harmonic Drive Replacement

steps.action: Power off robot completely via main switch; engage emergency stop lanyard; wait 90 seconds for capacitor discharge
steps.warning: Residual servo energy present — never assume joint is unpowered without full discharge wait

steps.action: Place robot in supine position on padded work surface; secure torso with nylon straps to prevent roll
Remove knee outer cover panel (4x M3 JIS-head screws); set aside in labeled container
Photograph cable routing inside knee housing before any disconnection
Disconnect actuator CAN bus connector (JST-GH 4-pin) and 48V power connector; apply cable labels
Insert limb support block under lower leg to unload knee joint; remove 8x M4 actuator mounting bolts in star pattern
Extract actuator module assembly from housing; place on clean anti-static mat
Using bearing press and appropriate mandrel, press out worn harmonic drive assembly; note orientation witness marks
Clean housing bore with lint-free cloth and isopropyl alcohol; inspect for scoring
Apply thin coat of Mobil SHC 632 grease to new flexspline tooth contact surfaces
Press in new harmonic drive aligned to witness marks; verify seating within 0.05mm using dial indicator
Reinstall actuator module; torque M4 bolts to 4.5 Nm in star pattern using calibrated torque wrench
Reconnect CAN and power cables per original routing; reinstall cover panel
Power on robot; run joint calibration and zero-offset routine via Unitree SDK; verify position error < 0.3 deg across full ROM

failure_modes.component: lithium battery pack — cell group voltage imbalance
failure_modes.symptom: reduced runtime below 45 minutes, BMS overcurrent fault codes, unexpected shutdown under load
failure_modes.root_cause: lithium cell group capacity divergence due to micro-dendrite formation from repeated high-current discharge during dynamic locomotion; BMS fails to balance fast enough under 15A+ draw

repair_protocol.title: Battery Pack Cell Group Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Power off robot; disconnect charger; wait 5 minutes before handling battery
steps.warning: Li-ion batteries are a fire hazard if punctured or shorted — wear insulated gloves throughout

steps.action: Remove battery module access panel (6x M3 screws, underside of torso)
Slide battery module out on guides; place on non-conductive surface
Connect battery analyzer to BMS diagnostic port (USB-C); log per-cell voltage and internal resistance
steps.tool: Hioki BT3554 battery tester or equivalent

steps.action: Identify cell groups with voltage delta > 50mV or internal resistance > 80mOhm vs nominal group average
If delta < 100mV: perform slow balance charge at 0.1C via BMS port and retest after 24 hours
steps.tool: bench power supply, BMS interface cable

steps.action: If balance charge fails to resolve delta: replace full battery module — do not attempt individual cell replacement in field
steps.warning: Cell-level replacement requires controlled environment and spot welder; not safe in field

steps.action: Install new OEM battery module; slide in on guides; reinstall access panel
Perform initial charge cycle to 100%; confirm BMS reports all cells within 10mV delta
steps.tool: OEM charger, laptop with BMS diagnostic software

failure_modes.component: ankle absolute encoder — magnetic position sensor
failure_modes.symptom: ankle joint reports erratic position readings at startup; controller throws encoder fault E03; robot fails self-check and refuses to stand
failure_modes.root_cause: magnetic absolute encoder (hall-effect type) experiences signal degradation from ferrous debris contamination near magnetic disc or from thermal cycling causing encoder PCB solder joint micro-cracking

repair_protocol.title: Ankle Absolute Encoder Replacement

steps.action: Power off; wait 90 seconds discharge; place robot supine on foam pad
Remove ankle outer shell (6x M3 JIS screws); expose encoder PCB
Photograph encoder orientation and zero-reference witness mark on magnetic disc
Disconnect encoder JST-SH 6-pin flex cable from controller board
Remove 2x M2 encoder PCB mounting screws; extract PCB
Inspect magnetic disc for ferrous debris; clean with IPA if contaminated
Install new encoder PCB; align to witness mark within +/-1 degree; torque M2 screws to 0.15 Nm
Reconnect flex cable; replace ankle shell; power on; run encoder calibration; verify fault E03 cleared

failure_modes.component: motor controller MOSFET bridge — arm joints
failure_modes.symptom: joint becomes unresponsive; controller reports overcurrent fault; MOSFET junction temperature alarm
failure_modes.root_cause: MOSFET H-bridge failure from repeated avalanche breakdown during aggressive trajectory following; thermal cycling degrades gate oxide

repair_protocol.title: Motor Controller MOSFET Bridge Replacement

steps.action: Power off; engage e-stop; wait 120 seconds; verify 0V on bus with multimeter before opening controller enclosure
steps.warning: DC bus may hold 48V+ for over 60 seconds — verify with meter before touching

steps.action: Remove arm shell panels to expose motor controller PCB module
Disconnect all connectors; label each; remove 4x M3 controller mounting standoffs
Using DMM diode mode, test all 6 MOSFET positions; failed devices show forward voltage < 0.2V in both directions
Replace entire controller module (field replacement unit)
Install new controller module; reconnect all connectors per photographs; torque standoffs
Flash firmware via SDK to match current robot version; run full joint ROM test
steps.tool: laptop with Unitree SDK, USB-C cable, clamp meter

parts.part_name: Unitree G1 Arm Motor Controller Module

failure_modes.component: head depth camera / structured light emitter
failure_modes.symptom: point cloud dropouts in 15-30 degree arc; SLAM drift accumulates faster than expected; obstacle detection failures
failure_modes.root_cause: VCSEL array partial failure from ESD damage or thermal stress; protective glass micro-scratches from debris reducing structured light contrast

repair_protocol.title: Head Perception Sensor Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Power off; remove head cover assembly (4x M3 screws)
Inspect protective glass for scratches under oblique-angle flashlight
Clean glass with dry sensor-safe lens cloth; if scratches present, proceed to replacement
steps.tool: lens cleaning cloth, sensor-safe air blower

steps.action: Power on; run depth sensor diagnostic via SDK; capture point cloud vs calibration target at 1m
steps.tool: laptop with SDK, calibration target board

steps.action: If dropout arc confirmed, disconnect sensor cable; remove 4x M2 mounting screws; extract sensor
Install replacement sensor; reconnect; run intrinsic and extrinsic calibration via SDK calibration wizard
steps.tool: laptop with SDK, checkerboard calibration target

steps.action: Verify SLAM drift < 2cm over 10m traverse; reinstall head cover

failure_modes.component: hip abduction harmonic drive — lateral load bearing
failure_modes.symptom: side-step gait shows lateral lean drift; hip abduction torque vs actual diverges by > 15%; clicking at mid-stance during lateral sway
failure_modes.root_cause: harmonic drive wave generator bearing failure (pitting) under combined axial and radial loading during lateral locomotion

repair_protocol.title: Hip Abduction Harmonic Drive Wave Generator Bearing Replacement

steps.action: Power off; wait 90 seconds; place robot in lateral recumbent position with affected hip up; support with foam cradle
Remove hip outer shell panels (6x M3 JIS screws); disconnect hip actuator CAN and 48V power connectors
Remove 10x M5 actuator flange bolts in star pattern; support leg weight
Extract hip actuator assembly; disassemble to expose wave generator sub-assembly
Remove retaining ring; extract wave generator from flexspline; press out elliptical bearing
steps.tool: snap-ring pliers, wave generator puller, bench press, bearing extraction mandrel

steps.action: Press in new elliptical bearing; apply Mobil SHC 632 grease to bearing outer race; reassemble wave generator
steps.tool: bench press, grease applicator, snap-ring pliers

steps.action: Reinstall actuator in hip; torque M5 flange bolts to 8.0 Nm in star pattern; reconnect cables
Power on; run hip calibration; verify torque tracking error < 5% across full abduction ROM

parts.part_name: Harmonic Drive CSF-20-100-2UH Wave Generator Sub-Assembly

failure_modes.component: Jetson Orin compute — thermal throttling
failure_modes.symptom: locomotion controller loop rate drops from 1kHz to 400Hz intermittently; gait becomes jerky; CPU temperature > 80C in ambient above 25C
failure_modes.root_cause: inadequate thermal interface material (TIM) contact between Jetson Orin and chassis heat spreader due to assembly torque variation or TIM pump-out over thermal cycles

repair_protocol.title: Jetson Orin Thermal Interface Material Refresh

steps.action: Power off; wait 5 minutes for SoC to cool below 40C
Remove torso back panel (8x M3 JIS screws) to expose compute bay
Photograph all cable and connector positions in compute bay
Remove Jetson Orin module retaining screws (4x M2.5); lift module straight up
steps.warning: Jetson SO-DIMM connector is fragile — lift straight up, do not rock

steps.action: Remove old TIM from both module lid and heat spreader using IPA and lint-free cloth
Apply thin even bead (0.2mm) of Shin-Etsu X-23 7783D to module lid; spread uniformly with card
Reseat Jetson module; reinstall retaining screws torqued to 0.25 Nm; reinstall torso panel
Power on; run 30-minute load test; verify Jetson temp stays below 70C at ambient 25C

failure_modes.component: CAN bus wiring harness — hip flex point chafing
failure_modes.symptom: intermittent joint dropouts on one leg; CAN bus error frame rate spikes > 100/second during full hip flex; fault clears at rest
failure_modes.root_cause: CAN bus twisted-pair wiring inside hip flex conduit experiences repeated bending stress; insulation chafing against conduit edge causes intermittent short correlating with joint angle

repair_protocol.title: Hip Flex Point CAN Bus Wiring Harness Repair

steps.action: Power off; trace CAN harness from hip controller to leg bus; remove hip shell panels
Flex hip joint manually through full ROM while monitoring CAN error counter on laptop; mark angle where error rate spikes
steps.tool: laptop with CAN bus analyzer (PEAK PCAN-USB)

steps.action: Inspect harness at outer flex radius for insulation abrasion
Cut harness 30mm either side of damage; splice in new twisted-pair segment using 26AWG shielded CAN cable
steps.tool: wire cutters, soldering iron, heat shrink

steps.action: Apply dual-wall adhesive heat shrink over each joint; verify shield continuity end-to-end with DMM
Reroute harness with additional bend radius protection sleeve at flex point; secure with nylon clips
steps.tool: nylon wire clips, flex conduit sleeve

steps.action: Reinstall shell panels; power on; flex hip through full ROM; verify CAN error rate < 1 error/minute

failure_modes.component: knee joint harmonic drive — high-torque flexspline
failure_modes.symptom: loud popping or crackling at knee during stair ascent; joint tracking error > 3 degrees under 30kg+ payload; visible lateral wobble in stance phase
failure_modes.root_cause: flexspline cup fatigue cracking from repeated high-torque knee extension under payload; H1-2 operates at higher joint torques than G1 due to greater mass and payload; inadequate lubrication re-interval compliance

repair_protocol.title: H1-2 Knee Harmonic Drive Full Module Replacement

steps.action: Power off via main switch; engage emergency stop; wait 120 seconds for 48V bus discharge — H1-2 has larger bus capacitors than G1
steps.warning: H1-2 capacitor bank holds charge longer — verify 0V with multimeter before proceeding

steps.action: Hoist robot to chest-high work stand using lift harness attachment points on torso; secure with ratchet straps
steps.tool: gantry hoist, lift harness, ratchet straps
steps.warning: H1-2 weighs 70kg — 2-person lift required; do not use floor position for this repair

steps.action: Remove knee outer shell (8x M4 JIS screws, 2 side + 2 front panels)
Photograph actuator cable routing and connector orientation
Disconnect actuator CAN (JST-GH 4-pin) and 48V power (XT60 variant) connectors; label both
Support lower leg with adjustable limb support sling; remove 12x M5 actuator flange bolts in star pattern
Extract full actuator module; weigh — nominal 2.8kg; log any deviation
Install new actuator module — H1-2 uses CSF-25-100-2UH-LW harmonic drive; align mounting holes and insert flange bolts finger-tight
Torque M5 flange bolts to 9.0 Nm in 3-pass star pattern
Reconnect CAN and power connectors; reinstall shell panels
Power on; run joint calibration sequence; verify tracking error < 0.5 deg under 15kg payload on knee
steps.tool: laptop with Unitree H1-2 SDK, calibration weights

failure_modes.component: 2000Wh lithium battery pack — thermal runaway risk under sustained high-current draw
failure_modes.symptom: battery surface temperature > 55C; BMS emergency shutoff; battery module swelling visible through vents; acrid smell
failure_modes.root_cause: H1-2 battery operates at higher sustained current during dynamic locomotion tasks; cell group imbalance allows weaker cells to experience localised lithium plating and exothermic micro-shorts under thermal stress

repair_protocol.title: H1-2 Battery Pack Emergency Swap and Inspection

steps.action: If robot BMS has triggered emergency shutoff, do NOT override — evacuate area and allow 30-minute cool-down before approach
steps.warning: Thermal runaway can re-ignite — have CO2 fire extinguisher standing by; do not use water on Li battery fire

steps.action: Verify robot surface temperature < 45C with thermal camera before handling
steps.tool: FLIR thermal camera or IR thermometer

steps.action: Remove battery module from torso bay (2x M8 quick-release captive bolts); slide module on rails out of chassis
Inspect module exterior for swelling deformation (> 1mm bulge = condemn)
Transfer condemned module to LiPo fire-safe bag immediately; tag for specialist disposal — do not charge
Install new OEM battery module; torque M8 bolts to 25 Nm; reconnect XT90 power connector and CAN diagnostic port
Connect BMS diagnostic cable; verify all cell groups within 20mV delta before first charge
steps.tool: laptop with H1-2 BMS diagnostic software

steps.action: Perform initial charge at 0.5C; monitor for 30 minutes; verify no temperature rise > 5C above ambient

failure_modes.component: wrist dexterous actuator — compact harmonic drive
failure_modes.symptom: hand/wrist loses grip precision; position overshoot > 5mm at end-effector; audible ratcheting at wrist during fine manipulation
failure_modes.root_cause: wrist compact harmonic drive (CSF-11 size) lubricant depletion; wrist operates at high duty cycle during manipulation tasks, accelerating grease consumption beyond service interval

repair_protocol.title: Wrist Harmonic Drive Lubrication Service

steps.action: Power off; place arm in horizontal relaxed position; remove wrist outer shell (4x M2.5 JIS screws)
Locate harmonic drive grease port (M3 zerk fitting); clean with IPA
Inject 0.5ml Mobil SHC 632 grease via grease gun; rotate wrist by hand through 5 full cycles to distribute
If ratcheting persists after lubrication, replace wrist actuator module (full swap, not field rebuildable)
Reinstall wrist shell; power on; run wrist calibration; verify grip position error < 1mm at 200mm extension
steps.tool: JIS #0 screwdriver, laptop with SDK, pin gauge set

failure_modes.component: IP54 torso sealing — cable gland and panel seam ingress
failure_modes.symptom: moisture detected by internal humidity sensor; corrosion visible on connector pins; CAN transceiver faults after outdoor operation in rain
failure_modes.root_cause: IP54 sealing depends on panel gaskets and cable gland compression; repeated thermal cycling and panel-remove/replace maintenance cycles degrade gasket compression and cable gland grip torque

repair_protocol.title: Torso IP54 Sealing Restoration

steps.action: Power off; remove all torso panels; inspect all gaskets for compression set, tears, or debris
Clean all gasket grooves with IPA; remove old gasket material; measure groove depth
Install new cut-to-length EPDM closed-cell foam gaskets; press into groove; ensure joints overlap 10mm
Inspect all cable glands; re-torque to manufacturer spec (typically M12 glands to 2.5 Nm); replace any with visible cracking
Apply thin bead of Dow Corning 732 RTV to any panel seams where gasket coverage is uncertain
Reinstall panels; torque panel screws to 1.2 Nm; perform IP54 water spray test per IEC 60529 with garden hose spray for 3 minutes
steps.tool: torque wrench, garden hose with adjustable spray nozzle

steps.action: Power on; check internal humidity sensor reading; verify < 60% RH

parts.part_name: EPDM Closed-Cell Foam Gasket Tape 10mm x 3mm x 5m

failure_modes.component: shoulder pitch actuator — motor phase winding
failure_modes.symptom: shoulder joint refuses commanded trajectory; motor controller reports phase current asymmetry > 20%; arm hangs limp or locks
failure_modes.root_cause: motor phase winding inter-turn short circuit from repeated insulation stress at high continuous current; H1-2 shoulder sees high continuous current during sustained arm-elevated manipulation tasks

repair_protocol.title: Shoulder Motor Phase Winding Fault Diagnosis and Module Replacement

steps.action: Power off; verify 0V bus; remove shoulder shell panels
Disconnect motor phase leads; measure winding resistance between each phase pair with LCR meter — expect 0.15-0.25 ohm; deviation > 30% indicates inter-turn short
steps.tool: Hioki LCR meter IM3536 or equivalent

steps.action: If inter-turn short confirmed, replace full actuator module — field winding repair not feasible without vacuum impregnation equipment
Extract shoulder actuator (12x M5 flange bolts); install new module; torque to 9.0 Nm in star pattern
Flash motor controller configuration for new actuator; run current balance test at 5A and 20A command; verify asymmetry < 3%
steps.tool: laptop with H1-2 SDK, clamp meter

failure_modes.component: foot force/torque sensor — overload from drop landing
failure_modes.symptom: foot contact force readings saturate or read zero; balance controller increases oscillation; walking speed reduced automatically by safety governor
failure_modes.root_cause: six-axis F/T sensor strain gauge bridge fracture from impulse overload during stair misstep or 30cm+ unintended drop; sensor rated for normal gait impulse, not fall impact

repair_protocol.title: Foot Force/Torque Sensor Replacement

steps.action: Power off; place robot supine; remove foot outer shell (6x M3 JIS screws)
Apply 0N reference to sensor cable output and verify all 6 axes read within 5N/0.5Nm of zero; if any axis saturates, sensor requires replacement
steps.tool: laptop with SDK, F/T calibration fixture

steps.action: Disconnect F/T sensor cable (JST-GH 8-pin); remove 6x M3 mounting bolts; extract sensor disc
Install new sensor; align orientation mark to chassis datum; torque M3 bolts to 2.5 Nm in star pattern
Run full F/T sensor calibration via SDK; apply known reference weights; verify < 2% error across all axes
steps.tool: laptop with H1-2 SDK, calibration weights 1kg-20kg

steps.action: Reinstall foot shell; power on full system; verify balance controller uses updated sensor readings

failure_modes.component: Jetson Orin NX compute — NVMe SSD failure
failure_modes.symptom: robot fails to boot; controller reports file system read error; in-field logs truncated or missing
failure_modes.root_cause: NVMe SSD write endurance exhaustion from high-frequency ROS2 bag recording and telemetry logging; M.2 2230 SSD endurance < 150TBW under sustained log workload

repair_protocol.title: Jetson Orin NX NVMe SSD Replacement and OS Restore

steps.action: Power off; access compute bay via torso back panel (8x M3 screws)
Locate M.2 2230 slot; remove single M2 retaining screw; extract SSD at 30-degree angle
Inspect SSD contacts for corrosion; install new Western Digital SN740 M.2 2230 512GB NVMe
steps.warning: Handle by edges — ESD damage will void warranty

steps.action: Boot robot with USB recovery drive prepared from H1-2 factory image; follow SDK flash procedure to restore OS and firmware
steps.tool: USB-A drive with H1-2 factory image, laptop

steps.action: After restore, configure log rotation to limit ROS2 bag write rate; verify SSD health with nvme-cli smart-log
steps.tool: laptop with SSH access to robot

failure_modes.component: ankle dorsiflexion harmonic drive
failure_modes.symptom: ankle joint position error exceeds 4 degrees during toe-off phase; robot adopts compensatory flat-footed gait; step height reduction on stairs
failure_modes.root_cause: ankle harmonic drive experiences highest position accuracy demand during toe-off; flexspline wear from high-frequency cyclical loading at near-peak torque in push-off phase

repair_protocol.title: Ankle Harmonic Drive Replacement

steps.action: Power off; 120 second discharge wait; place robot supine; support leg with foam cradle
Remove ankle shell (6x M3 JIS screws); disconnect actuator CAN and power connectors
Remove 8x M4 actuator mounting bolts; extract ankle actuator module
Disassemble to harmonic drive; replace flexspline and wave generator bearing; regrease
steps.tool: snap-ring pliers, bearing press, Mobil SHC 632, grease syringe

steps.action: Reassemble and reinstall ankle actuator; torque M4 bolts to 4.5 Nm; reconnect cables
Run ankle calibration and gait trial; verify position error < 1 deg during toe-off at normal walking speed

failure_modes.component: knee harmonic drive — IP67 internal water ingress post-immersion
failure_modes.symptom: joint torque ripple increases after water immersion; harmonic drive grease appears milky on inspection; gait asymmetry on wet terrain
failure_modes.root_cause: IP67 rating requires intact shaft seals and housing o-rings; seal compression degrades after 500+ immersion cycles; water contamination of harmonic drive grease causes lubricant film breakdown and accelerated flexspline wear

repair_protocol.title: B2 Knee Actuator IP67 Seal Inspection and Grease Flush

steps.action: Power off; engage emergency stop; wait 90 seconds; lay robot on side with affected leg up
Remove leg outer shell panels (6x M4 JIS screws); expose knee actuator housing
Remove IP67 shaft seal cover (3x M3 bolts); inspect o-ring for compression set or cracking
steps.warning: Replacement o-ring must be EPDM 70 Shore A — do not substitute NBR which swells with Mobil SHC 632 grease

steps.action: If o-ring damaged: remove with pick tool; clean groove with IPA; install new EPDM o-ring with thin coat of Molykote 55 o-ring lubricant
Extract grease via drain port; inspect for milky appearance (water contamination) or gray metallic particles (wear debris)
Flush cavity with clean Mobil SHC 632 via injection port; rotate joint 10 full cycles; extract and discard flush grease
Fill with fresh Mobil SHC 632 to 80% cavity fill; reinstall seal cover; torque M3 bolts to 2.5 Nm
Perform IP67 immersion test: submerge knee joint to 1m depth for 30 minutes; verify no water entry
Power on; run full gait test on level terrain; verify torque ripple < 5% above nominal

parts.part_name: EPDM O-Ring ID18mm x 2.5mm Section (actuator shaft seal)

failure_modes.component: foot contact switch and pressure pad — abrasion wear
failure_modes.symptom: gait controller logs missing foot contact events; robot over-extends leg on hard surfaces; stance phase timing errors
failure_modes.root_cause: foot rubber pad and embedded contact switch degrade from abrasive terrain contact; B2 designed for outdoor industrial use on gravel/concrete which accelerates foot pad wear beyond 500-hour interval

repair_protocol.title: Foot Contact Pad and Switch Replacement

steps.action: Power off; inspect all four foot pads; measure rubber thickness with calipers — replace at < 5mm remaining
Remove foot outer housing (4x M3 screws); extract rubber pad and contact switch assembly
Test contact switch continuity with DMM; verify actuation force with spring scale
Install new foot pad and switch assembly; apply medium-strength Loctite 243 to M3 mounting screws
Reinstall foot housing; power on; run contact calibration; verify all 4 feet trigger at < 10N force
steps.tool: laptop with B2 SDK, spring scale

parts.part_name: Unitree B2 Foot Pad and Contact Switch Kit

failure_modes.component: LTE/Wi-Fi communication module — antenna delamination
failure_modes.symptom: intermittent loss of remote operation and telemetry; LTE signal drops from -80dBm to -110dBm; connection drops correlate with body flexion motion
failure_modes.root_cause: LTE antenna is bonded to interior body panel; repeated body torsion during agile locomotion causes adhesive bond failure between antenna ground plane and panel substrate, reducing signal coupling

repair_protocol.title: LTE Antenna Rebond and RSSI Verification

steps.action: Power off; remove body top panel (6x M4 screws); locate LTE patch antenna on inner panel surface
Flex body panel manually; observe antenna; any visible edge lifting confirms delamination
Clean antenna back and panel surface with IPA; allow 5-minute dry time
Apply 3M 467MP adhesive transfer tape to antenna back; press antenna to panel with 5kg pressure for 60 seconds
Verify U.FL coaxial connector is fully seated on antenna port; re-mate if necessary
steps.tool: U.FL seating tool or blunt plastic pry

steps.action: Reinstall panel; power on; check LTE RSSI in diagnostics — expect > -90dBm in outdoor environment

failure_modes.component: hip abduction motor — bearing failure under lateral impact
failure_modes.symptom: hip abduction angle limited to < 15 degrees; robot adopts narrow-stance gait; increased current draw in hip abduction motor; grinding noise on side-step
failure_modes.root_cause: hip abduction BLDC motor uses single-row deep-groove bearing rated for primarily radial loads; lateral falls or side impacts transfer axial impulse loads exceeding bearing design limits; race brinelling initiates spalling

repair_protocol.title: Hip Abduction Motor Bearing Replacement

steps.action: Power off; lay robot on side with affected hip up; remove hip abduction shell (8x M4 screws)
Disconnect motor phase wires and encoder cable; remove motor from abduction joint (6x M4 flange bolts)
Disassemble motor; press out rotor from front bearing; inspect for brinelling (dimples in race)
Press in new FAG 6206-2Z angular contact bearing rated for combined loading; apply Mobilgrease 28 to bearing
Reassemble motor; reinstall to hip joint; torque M4 bolts to 5.0 Nm; reconnect phase and encoder
Run hip abduction calibration; verify full ROM ±35 degrees; verify current draw < 8A at max ROM
steps.tool: laptop with B2 SDK, clamp meter

failure_modes.component: LiDAR mid-360 — window fouling from mud and dust
failure_modes.symptom: point cloud density drops 30-50%; obstacle detection range reduced; false positive obstacle detections from partial returns
failure_modes.root_cause: B2 operates close to ground in industrial/outdoor environments; LiDAR rotating prism window accumulates mud splash, concrete dust, and moisture film within 4-8 hours in dirty environments; window has no self-cleaning mechanism

repair_protocol.title: LiDAR Window Cleaning and Verification

steps.action: Power off robot; inspect LiDAR window under oblique light for mud or film deposits
Dampen lens cleaning cloth with methanol or IPA; wipe window in single radial strokes; do not apply circular motion (can create micro-scratches)
steps.warning: Never use compressed air directly on LiDAR window — particulates can embed in lens coating

steps.action: Inspect for remaining debris with oblique light; repeat with fresh cloth section if needed
Power on; run LiDAR health diagnostic; verify point count > 95% nominal in open environment
steps.tool: laptop with B2 diagnostics, open area 10m clearance

steps.action: If cleaning does not restore point count, inspect for scratches; scratched window requires replacement of LiDAR sensor assembly

parts.part_name: Unitree B2 LiDAR Sensor Assembly (if window scratched)

failure_modes.component: battery 2000Wh — cold weather capacity derating
failure_modes.symptom: runtime drops below 2 hours in ambient < 5C; BMS reports high internal resistance; robot terminates mission early with battery > 20% SOC
failure_modes.root_cause: lithium NMC chemistry experiences significant capacity and power derating below 10C ambient; B2 designed for industrial outdoor use including cold climates; BMS thermal management system does not actively heat battery during storage

repair_protocol.title: Cold Weather Battery Pre-Conditioning Protocol

steps.action: If ambient < 5C, connect charger for 30 minutes before deployment to allow BMS pre-heat cycle
Verify battery surface temp > 15C via BMS telemetry before beginning mission
If battery surface remains < 10C after 30-minute charge: apply insulated battery wrap (neoprene pouch rated for operating temperature)
Reduce mission SOC cutoff from 20% to 30% in fleet management settings for ambient < 5C operation
After mission, charge immediately while battery is warm — do not store discharged in cold

parts.part_name: Neoprene Battery Insulating Pouch (custom cut for B2 battery module)

specs_json.compute: Intel Core i5 (arm computer), dedicated motor controllers

failure_modes.component: series elastic actuator (SEA) spring pack — fatigue fracture
failure_modes.symptom: joint position control becomes sluggish with 20-40ms lag; torque sensing reads erratic; gait contact timing errors logged; robot triggers fall prevention and halts
failure_modes.root_cause: SEA steel spring pack experiences high-cycle metal fatigue from repeated locomotion impacts; Spot designed for up to 1 million steps — spring pack fatigue initiates at 500k steps in high-terrain use; fracture renders SEA force sensing unreliable

repair_protocol.title: SEA Spring Pack Inspection and Module Replacement

steps.action: Power off; engage e-stop; wait 120 seconds for all actuators to discharge stored spring energy
steps.warning: SEA actuators store mechanical energy in compressed springs — never disassemble under load or immediately after power-off

steps.action: Position robot on padded work stand in standing configuration at working height; support each leg with foam blocks to unload joints
Remove leg outer shell panels per Boston Dynamics service manual; expose SEA housing
Connect Spot tablet/laptop to robot API; run actuator diagnostics — `spring_deflection_variance` > 0.8mm RMS indicates spring pack fatigue
steps.tool: Boston Dynamics Spot API (Python SDK), laptop

steps.action: If spring pack fatigue confirmed: remove SEA module (6x M5 bolts); weigh and log serial number
Install factory-refurbished SEA module from Boston Dynamics spare parts program; align spline shaft carefully
steps.warning: Boston Dynamics does not support field spring-pack-level repair — full SEA module swap only

steps.action: Torque M5 bolts to 8.0 Nm in star pattern; reconnect SEA CAN connector
Run Boston Dynamics commissioning script via API; verify `spring_deflection_variance` < 0.3mm RMS; perform 100-step gait trial

tools_required: laptop with Boston Dynamics Spot API

parts.part_name: Boston Dynamics Spot SEA Module (factory refurbished)

failure_modes.component: 605Wh battery pack — cell degradation and connector erosion
failure_modes.symptom: runtime drops below 45 minutes; docking station reports charge fault; battery LED shows orange (fault); CLI `battery info` shows SOH < 70%
failure_modes.root_cause: lithium cells degrade faster under repeated partial-discharge cycles during multi-shift operation without full charge cycles; battery dock contact pins experience arcing erosion from repeated mate/demate under partial charge residual current

repair_protocol.title: Spot Battery Pack and Dock Contact Replacement

steps.action: Log battery SOH via robot SDK: `robot_state.power_state.locomotion_charge_percentage` and battery info endpoint
Inspect dock contact pins (4-pin, gold-plated) for pitting or blackening; inspect matching battery receptacle
Clean dock pins with contact cleaner and cotton swab; if pitting depth > 0.5mm, replace dock contact assembly
steps.tool: DeoxIT D5 contact cleaner, cotton swab, vernier calipers

steps.action: Press battery release on robot side; slide battery out of bay; install new OEM battery
Perform 3 full charge cycles (0-100%) to condition new cells; verify SOH > 95% after conditioning
Dispose of old battery per Boston Dynamics battery return program; do not landfill

failure_modes.component: IP54 hip panel seal — degradation from cleaning agent exposure
failure_modes.symptom: internal moisture alarm; corrosion on payload bay connector pins; IP54 rating compromised after industrial environment deployment
failure_modes.root_cause: Spot deployed in industrial inspection environments uses harsh cleaning agents (degreasers, alkaline foam cleaners) for decontamination; some agents degrade EPDM panel gaskets and silicone RTV seals faster than water alone

repair_protocol.title: Hip Panel IP54 Gasket Replacement and Corrosion Treatment

steps.action: Power off; remove all payload equipment; remove hip panels per Boston Dynamics service guide (T20 Torx, 12 screws)
Inspect all gaskets and RTV beads for chemical attack (swelling, crumbling, discoloration)
Remove all degraded gasket material; clean channels with IPA
Treat any corrosion on connector pins with DeoxIT D5; dry with compressed air
steps.warning: Do not use isopropyl alcohol on gold connector pins — causes micro-pitting

steps.action: Install new silicone gasket tape rated for chemical resistance (EPDM or PTFE-backed); press firmly into groove
Apply thin bead of chemical-resistant RTV (Dow Corning 736 Heat Resistant Sealant) to seam areas
Reinstall panels; torque T20 screws to 2.5 Nm; perform water spray test; monitor humidity sensor < 60% RH
steps.tool: torque wrench, garden hose spray nozzle, SDK humidity monitor

parts.part_name: PTFE-Backed Self-Adhesive Gasket Tape 8mm x 2mm x 3m

failure_modes.component: motor controller board — thermal derating from blocked airflow
failure_modes.symptom: robot reduces gait speed automatically; SDK reports motor_temp_warning on 1-2 legs; performance restoration after 5-minute rest period
failure_modes.root_cause: Spot motor controllers rely on passive conduction cooling through leg housing; dust, mud, or debris buildup on external housing surface reduces thermal conductivity; controller reduces maximum torque output to protect electronics

repair_protocol.title: Motor Controller Housing External Thermal Surface Cleaning

steps.action: Power off; allow 10-minute cool-down; inspect all leg housing external surfaces for mud or debris buildup
Use stiff nylon brush to loosen dried mud/debris from heat dissipation fins and housing ribs
Wipe all surfaces with damp cloth; dry with compressed air blast
steps.warning: Do not aim compressed air at joint seals — may force debris past IP54 gaskets

steps.action: Power on; run 10-minute walk; verify motor temp stays < 60C via SDK temp readings
steps.tool: laptop with Spot SDK, open environment

steps.action: If thermal issue persists after cleaning, apply thin layer of Fujipoly XR-m gap filler between motor controller and housing wall
steps.tool: Fujipoly XR-m thermal gap pad, plastic scraper

failure_modes.component: foot rubber pad — wear and delamination
failure_modes.symptom: traction loss on smooth or wet surfaces; robot slips during direction changes; increased fall protection interventions; foot pad visually worn or delaminated
failure_modes.root_cause: foot rubber pads rated for ~1 million steps on typical mixed-terrain; concrete, asphalt, and industrial tile surfaces wear pads 3-5x faster; worn pads expose plastic foot structure causing coefficient of friction drop from 0.8 to 0.3

repair_protocol.title: Spot Foot Rubber Pad Replacement

steps.action: Measure rubber pad center thickness with calipers — replace at < 4mm remaining (nominal 12mm)
Remove foot from leg: remove 3x M4 foot-to-lower-leg bolts; disconnect foot (no internal connectors on standard foot)
Peel old rubber pad from foot structure; clean bonding surface with IPA
Apply Loctite 480 rubber bonding adhesive to foot structure; press new pad; clamp for 2 hours
Reinstall foot to leg; torque M4 bolts to 4.0 Nm
Run 5-minute walk trial on hard surface; verify no slipping during sharp turns

parts.part_name: Boston Dynamics Spot Foot Rubber Pad Set (4x)

failure_modes.component: Spot Core payload computer — NVMe SSD endurance
failure_modes.symptom: Spot Core fails to boot; user payload apps unavailable; SSH connection timeout; Core LED shows error pattern
failure_modes.root_cause: NVMe SSD write endurance exhausted from continuous payload application log writing; Spot Core Intel NUC-based compute logs at high rate during inspection missions; M.2 SSD rated 150TBW exceeded after 18-24 months of daily use

repair_protocol.title: Spot Core NVMe SSD Replacement

steps.action: Remove Spot Core from payload bay (4x M4 captive bolts); disconnect Ethernet and power cable
Open Spot Core enclosure per service guide (6x T8 Torx screws)
Remove M.2 2280 NVMe SSD (single M2 screw); install new Samsung 990 Pro 1TB M.2 NVMe
Restore Spot Core OS from USB recovery image; follow Boston Dynamics Spot Core restore procedure
steps.tool: USB-A recovery drive, monitor/keyboard via HDMI

steps.action: Configure log rotation limits; verify SSD SMART health shows available_spare > 90%; reinstall Core to robot

failure_modes.component: wrist / gripper attachment interface — electrical contact fouling
failure_modes.symptom: gripper payload loses power intermittently; payload detection fails; SDK shows payload not authenticated; gripper opens unexpectedly mid-task
failure_modes.root_cause: Spot payload port uses spring-loaded pogo pin contacts for power and data; industrial environments deposit conductive dust and moisture on pogo pins and mating PCB pads; contact resistance rises above authentication threshold

repair_protocol.title: Spot Gripper/Payload Pogo Pin Contact Cleaning

steps.action: Power off robot; remove gripper from wrist payload port
Inspect wrist pogo pins (12-pin array) and gripper mating pads under magnification; look for oxidation or debris
Apply 1-2 drops DeoxIT D5 to pogo pin contact surface; mate and demate gripper 10x to burnish contacts
Dry with compressed air; wipe mating pads on gripper with IPA-dampened cotton swab
Reattach gripper; power on; verify payload authentication succeeds; run grip/open 20 cycles via tablet app
If contact resistance still high after cleaning, inspect pogo pin spring tension — replace pogo pin block if spring force < 50g measured with spring scale
steps.tool: spring scale, pogo pin replacement block

parts.part_name: Boston Dynamics Spot Wrist Pogo Pin Contact Block

failure_modes.component: front stereo camera pair — calibration drift from thermal shock
failure_modes.symptom: 3D vision obstacle detection range reduces from 5m to 2m; robot takes wider berth around obstacles than commanded; navigation errors in tight spaces
failure_modes.root_cause: Spot front stereo cameras require sub-pixel calibration for accurate depth estimation; rapid temperature changes (indoor/outdoor transitions in winter) cause aluminum camera bracket thermal expansion mismatch with camera module, shifting stereo baseline by 50-100 microns — sufficient to corrupt depth accuracy

repair_protocol.title: Front Stereo Camera Recalibration

steps.action: Allow robot to equilibrate to ambient temperature for 30 minutes before calibration — never calibrate immediately after temperature transition
Set up Boston Dynamics stereo calibration target (1m x 1m checkerboard) at 2m from robot in consistent lighting
steps.tool: Boston Dynamics calibration checkerboard, even lighting

steps.action: Connect laptop to robot via Wi-Fi; run calibration tool via Spot SDK: `bosdyn.client.image_service` calibration routine
Follow SDK calibration wizard — capture 15-20 positions; wizard will reject positions with insufficient baseline variation
Apply new calibration; verify obstacle detection range > 4.5m on 100mm calibration target
steps.tool: Spot SDK depth verification script, measuring tape

suppliers.name: Boston Dynamics Authorized Service
suppliers.notes: Official parts only — no third-party SEA modules supported
